dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9299 IH: Advancing Next-Generation Genetic Learning for Effective Resource Strategy Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Scholten introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Natural Resources

form.official-title: To direct the Secretary of the Interior to establish a grant program to award amounts to eligible entities to develop and carry out programs to incorporate eDNA monitoring techniques into and use eDNA monitoring techniques in carrying out sport fish surveys, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Advancing Next-Generation Genetic Learning for Effective Resource Strategy Act.

section.header: eDNA sport fish survey grant program

subsection.text: The Secretary, in coordination with the Secretary of Commerce, acting through the Administrator of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, and Sport Fishing and Boating Partnership Council, shall establish a grant program to award amounts to eligible entities to develop and carry out programs to incorporate eDNA monitoring techniques into and use eDNA monitoring techniques in carrying out sport fish surveys, to be known as the eDNA Sport Fish Survey grant program.

To be eligible for a grant under the grant program, an eligible entity shall submit to the Secretary an application in such form, at such time, and containing such information as the Secretary determines appropriate.

subsection.header: State and tribal fish and wildlife agencies
subsection.text: In carrying out the grant program, the Secretary shall encourage each eligible entity that is awarded a grant under the grant program to collaborate and work in coordination with relevant State and Tribal fish and wildlife agencies.

An eligible entity that is awarded a grant under the grant program shall use such grant to develop and carry out a program to incorporate eDNA monitoring techniques into and use eDNA monitoring techniques in carrying out sport fish surveys in order to—

paragraph.text: increase the spatial scale of the sampling of sport fish surveys across broader geographic areas, including under surveyed or remote regions;
increase the frequency of sport fish surveys for improved accuracy of population count and species distribution for management;
enhance the accuracy of species identification and population estimates of sport fish;
improve the availability of data for sport fish populations to inform fisheries management and conservation practices;
monitor species listed as endangered species under section 4 of the Endangered Species Act of 1973 (16 U.S.C. 1533).

subsection.text: In awarding grants under the grant program, the Secretary shall give priority to applications from eligible entities that demonstrate the intent of the eligible entity to collaborate with other eligible entities in using any awarded amounts.

In carrying out the grant program, the Secretary shall, to the extent practicable, identify and collect data that informs objective metrics of the efficacy of each program carried out by each eligible entity that is awarded a grant under the grant program.
There is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary to carry out this section $4,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2027 through 2033.

paragraph.text: The term eDNA means genetic material, such as DNA or RNA, that is collected from an environmental sample of water or sediment.

subparagraph.text: a State, local, or Tribal government;
an institution of higher education; and

paragraph.text: The term grant program means the eDNA Sport Fish Survey grant program established under subsection (a).
The term institution of higher education has the meaning given the term in section 101 of the Higher Education Act of 1965 (20 U.S.C. 1001).
The term Secretary means the Secretary of the Interior, acting through the Director of the United States Geological Survey.
